text.title: Django Project Structure and Import Conventions
text.summary: Standard Django project layout, aliased cross-app import rules, and settings-split conventions that prevent circular imports and misconfiguration.

rule.statement: Organise Django projects as: config/ (settings split), apps/ (domain applications), core/ (shared BaseModel + exceptions), tests/ (conftest.py).
rule.rationale: Separating config from apps from shared code gives each a single reason to change and makes the project onboardable by agents without reading every file.

example.code: project/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ settings/ в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ base.py # secrets-free defaults в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ development.py # overrides only в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ production.py # overrides only в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ urls.py в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ celery.py в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ users/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ orders/ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ catalog/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ core/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ models.py # BaseModel в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ exceptions.py в””в”Ђв”Ђ tests/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ conftest.py

rule.statement: Always use module aliases for cross-app imports: from apps.orders import models as order_models. Never import symbols directly from another app's module.
rule.rationale: Direct cross-app symbol imports create circular import risk as apps grow. Aliased module imports let Django's app loading resolve the graph correctly.

example.code:
# Cross-app: always use alias from apps.orders import models as order_models from apps.users import services as user_services
# Own app: relative imports are fine from .models import User from . import constants from .services import create_user
# BAD: direct symbol import from another app from apps.orders.models import Order
example.note: This creates a direct dependency on apps.orders.models at import time. As apps grow, this pattern causes circular import errors that are hard to debug.

rule.statement: base.py is the only settings file with secrets-free defaults. development.py and production.py only override. Never import the other direction.
Never import from django.contrib.auth.models import User. Always use get_user_model() or settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL.
rule.rationale: Direct User import breaks custom user model setups silently. get_user_model() respects AUTH_USER_MODEL at runtime.
